description_long: This object is a short, L-shaped tunnel segment constructed from what appears to be reinforced concrete or metal, with a rough, industrial finish. The tunnel has a large, open, circular cross-section, suitable for the passage of medium-sized vehicles, utility lines, or drainage. One end of the tunnel is cleanly cut and open, while the other end features a perpendicular extension, possibly for connection to another tunnel or infrastructure. There are two protruding, rectangular support or mounting structures attached to the main body, suggesting it is designed to be anchored or integrated into a larger system. The surface is predominantly gray, with visible textural variation indicating a utilitarian, functional design. No lighting fixtures, signage, or decorative elements are present, emphasizing its practical, infrastructural purpose.
description: A short, L-shaped, industrial tunnel segment with a large circular opening and two rectangular support extensions, finished in rough gray material.
description_view_0: The tunnel's long, straight section is visible with a prominent right-angle bend and two rectangular support structures attached.
description_view_1: The open, circular end of the tunnel is seen head-on, with one support structure extending horizontally across the view.
description_view_2: The tunnel's L-shape is clear, showing the open end and the perpendicular extension, with both support structures visible.
description_view_3: The perpendicular extension and the open, circular end are prominent, with the support structure crossing the view horizontally.
attribute_entrance_type: Circular open ends, constructed from reinforced concrete or metal, with no doors or gates and a utilitarian finish.
attribute_surrounding_infrastructure: Rectangular support extensions suggest integration with adjacent infrastructure such as retaining walls, roadbeds, or utility corridors.
